mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.5 percent trend compresses equity risk premiums by confirming durable demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in expansion periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid labor market deterioration exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals an imminent earnings recession, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power in post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous federal funds rate increases paired with consumer price inflation exceeding 2.5 percent annually force real discount rates higher, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity valuations expand despite rising real rates and elevated inflation, or the joint signal yields a negative Rank IC across all rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth outpacing capacity utilization thresholds confirms supply-side efficiency gains that boost corporate margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days during recovery phases.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain flat or negative when industrial production accelerates above trend, or the signal fails to predict returns in any recovery period since 1990.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly housing starts exceeding 1.0 million units annually reflect robust consumer confidence and construction sector momentum, providing a positive earnings catalyst that lifts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Housing starts consistently exceed 1.0 million units while S&P 500 forward returns are negative, or the signal exhibits zero correlation with equity performance in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in residential fixed asset spending exceeding 5 percent over two months indicates severe housing sector distress, which transmits to broader equity markets through wealth effects and construction supply chains, depressing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive despite severe residential fixed asset drawdowns, or the threshold signal produces a positive Rank IC in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent nonfarm payroll growth exceeding 200,000 monthly alongside an unemployment rate below 5.5 percent validates a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that sustains consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets decline despite strong payroll gains and low unemployment, or the joint labor signal yields a negative predictive rank correlation over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes implemented during a zero lower bound environment trigger immediate repricing of duration risk, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days as markets adjust to normalized policy trajectories.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive following rate hikes at the zero lower bound, or the signal shows no statistical significance in forward return prediction during post-2008 periods.
